title: Daily Briefing — Tuesday, May 19
tldr: Play: wire one MCP server into your AI tool so it acts on your real data. The pattern of the week was that every lab now ships a runtime, not just a model — and the durable skill is connecting your AI to your tools.
The keynote announcements were news; the run-it-today move is the MCP connection below.

sections.h: Run it today: connect your AI to your tools with MCP

blocks.sub: Stop copy-pasting between your AI and your apps
blocks.p: Payoff: your assistant can read a Google Drive doc, open a GitHub PR, or pull a Slack thread directly — instead of you ferrying text back and forth. MCP is 'a USB-C port for AI apps': connect once, use everywhere.
blocks.doIt: Pick one tool you touch daily (GitHub, Drive, Slack, a database). Add its MCP server to your AI client (Claude, Cursor, and VS Code all support MCP). Start read-only / scoped, confirm it fetches real data, then let it take one low-risk action. Directory and how-to: modelcontextprotocol.io; in Claude Code: Claude Code · MCP.
blocks.note: For sensitive data, scope permissions tightly and prefer local/VPC execution over sending data to a vendor cloud.

sources: Model Context Protocol · Claude Code · MCP
